name: J for JOKER | Express Through Clowning
description: Let's throw a tantrum,Let's move uninhibited like a child.Let's laugh out the failures,Let's be happy enough to cry.This Sunday, step out, play, move, and gift yourself a pocket of time. J for JOKER, a clowning workshop facilitated by Sheetal, is a space to feel and express the extremes to arrive at a balance.Who is it for?If you feel like you have trouble expressing or are often told you express too much, this is your space.The workshop is beginner-friendly, no experience in theatre or performance is required.If you are fascinated by clowning and would like to try it out, you better register.This It is a great way to meet like-minded individuals.

name: A nostalgic Baithak on childhood comics!
description: Many of us have spent our youth with our nose buried deep inside Amar Chitra Katha, Tinkle, Champak & other such comic books. These comics captivated us with their fantastical stories & intricate artwork, & often became our first source of contact with South Asian history & mythology. Though we've seen these stories adapted for television & film, nothing ever could quite capture the magic of the graphic novels we grew up on. 📚🌷 Silsila Heritage, invites you to our Baithak to reminisce on these comics & share our childhood experience with Amar Chitra Katha, Panchatantra & Tinkle. We will also be discussing how history & mythology is portrayed in Children's media, & how that influences our understanding of culture, identity & heritage. Along the way, we'll understand how exactly these comics were created & how this process shaped the stories being narrated.Whether you had a favorite Tinkle character, a beloved tale of ACK, or still remember the animals from Panchatantra, this Baithak is a space to revisit them all together. 🐯Know Your Baithak Host:✨️ Ananya is currently pursuing a Master’s in History from the University of Delhi, India, with a specialization in modern history. Her research interests primarily include caste, the colonial legal system & gendered labor in the colonial Indian context.

As the layers begin to soften, a natural state of calm, clarity, and inner healing emerges.This experience supports:✨ Emotional release and energetic healing✨ Deep nervous system relaxation✨ Letting go of fear, control, and inner resistance✨ Reconnecting with a more centred, grounded stateThe Mahamrityunjaya mantra carries a deeper significance of release from inner limitations and restoration of balance withinWith 108 repetitions, the sound creates a meditative rhythm that aligns breath, body, and awareness — allowing healing to unfold naturally, without force.

Curated by Ayandrali Dutta, food writer and culinary commentator deeply rooted in her Bengali heritage, this menu brings together the many layers of Bengali cuisine.. from nostalgic beginnings like churmur chaat and vegetable chop to comforting classics like luchi, kosha mangsho, and chingri malaikari, ending with nolen gur payesh.A celebration of renewal, togetherness, and everything that makes Bengali cuisine what it is.

name: Mehrauli Heritage Walk- A city within city!
description: Discover Mehrauli, Delhi's oldest inhabited neighborhood, home to the first city of Delhi, Lal Kot, and over 100 monuments spanning 1,000 years from Tomar Rajputs to Mughals. Join me, an architect and urban designer, on April 18th for an immersive walk through Mehrauli Archaeological Park's hidden gems: ancient tombs like Balban's, stepwells such as Rajon ki Baoli, Jamali-Kamali Mosque, and vibrant stories of layered histories. Experience the magic of this vast heritage hub, blending architecture, culture, and untold tales of Delhi's "city within a city."

name: Earth & Rhythm: Drum Circle
description: The Earth has its own music… have you been listening?Step into a space where rhythm becomes connection, and sound becomes a shared experience. Rhythm & Earth is not just a drum circle—it’s a guided, immersive journey into collective energy, expression, and presence.In this 60-minute experience, you’ll enter a circle of like-minded individuals and co-create rhythms together—no prior musical experience needed. As the beats align, something shifts: barriers soften, energy opens, and a natural sense of harmony begins to flow.This is where you don’t just play music… you feel it.Each session is intuitively guided yet organically shaped by the group, making every circle a unique and living experience. Whether you come to unwind, connect, or simply explore something new, this is your invitation to drop into rhythm and return to yourself.

location.address: E5, Market Rd, Block E, DLF Phase 1, Sector 26A, Gurugram, Haryana 122002, India

name: Metcalfe’s Mehrauli: A World Heritage Day Walk
description: There’s something about Mehrauli at dusk that changes the way you listen to a place.On the evening of 19th April, a day after World Heritage Day, join us for a sunset walk from 6:00 to 7:30 pm at the quiet Quli Khan’s Tomb. A structure that has watched centuries pass in silence, it sits within a landscape that would later be shaped into the Metcalfe Estate under Sir Thomas Metcalfe.We begin as the light softens and the park slowly exhales the day. It's experiencing heritage in the truest sense. We’ll sit, talk, and listen.The conversation will move through Delhi and Mehrauli as they shift over time, with a closer look at the 19th century. A period when older Mughal rhythms were giving way to new colonial ways of seeing and inhabiting the city. We’ll trace how landscapes were reused, how meanings changed, and how Mehrauli became both a retreat and a reimagined past.As evening settles in, the surroundings shift. The arches darken, the sky deepens, and the stillness becomes part of the experience. What this offers is not just history, but a certain mood. One that allows you to feel the weight of the place, rather than simply observe it.Please note: Entry tickets for the café and the tomb are not included and will need to be purchased separately.

location.address: Anuvrat Marg, opposite Qutab Minar Metro Station, Christian Colony, Mehrauli, New Delhi, Delhi 110030, India

Using soft fabric hammocks, kids will stretch, swing, invert, and balance—building strength, flexibility, and focus while having fun.More than just a class, this is a guided experience that encourages body awareness, courage, and self-trust. Moving through the air and exploring new positions helps children gently step out of their comfort zones in a safe and supportive environment.The session is curated to feel both playful and structured—so whether your child is new to aerial yoga or already active, they will feel comfortable, engaged, and inspired throughout.✨ What makes this specialA unique blend of movement, play, and mindful awareness—designed to help kids feel stronger, more confident, and connected to their body in a joyful way.

name: Paint Your Favourite Childhood Characters
description: Ever noticed how the characters you grew up with never really leave you? They stay tucked somewhere between memory and feeling : familiar, comforting, and a little magical.In this workshop, you’ll be painting your favourite childhood characters and turn them into fridge magnets : not just as fun objects, but as tiny artworks that bring a piece of your childhood into your everyday space.You can also join in as a pair, which means double the fun !! Each of you will paint two characters, creating your own little set of nostalgic magnets together.This workshop is part of Impressions, a buzzing Art event where six artists are coming together to share their art, processes, and stories ; so expect inspiration floating all around you while you draw!Bring a bit of nostalgia, and let’s turn it into something you’ll see, smile at, and keep close.With the energy of an art event around you, this workshop is less about perfection and more about joy, memory, and creating something that feels like you.🎨 About the WorkshopWhat’s happening?A hands-on session where you’ll paint your favourite characters and turn them into fridge magnets.Why join?Because some memories deserve to live beyond your screen ;right on your fridge.Vibe check:Playful, colourful, nostalgic, and full of feel-good energy. Come alone or come with a friend , nostalgia is more fun when shared.Skills required:None at all. Just bring your love for cartoons and a little curiosity.Materials:All materials will be provided. Each participant will get to paint TWO Fridge magnets!Character references are for inspiration only. You can choose what you'd like to paint from the collection.InstructorChhavi Bhatnagar is an artist whose work feels like a hug from memory. With a background in Architecture and Product Design, she brings an impressionistic and deeply personal approach to her art, drawing from childhood experiences, everyday moments, and the emotional layers of growing up.

location.address: No.1, Hauz Khas Village, Deer Park, Hauz Khas, New Delhi, Delhi 110016, India

name: Correlation Is Not Causation
description: Join us for a lecture that begins with a deceptively simple question:When two things move together, does one actually cause the other?About the LectureWe live in a world overflowing with data.Patterns are everywhere — ice cream sales rise, and so do drowning rates. But does one cause the other? Or are we just seeing coincidence dressed up as meaning?This lecture unpacks why economists insist, almost stubbornly, that correlation is not causation — and why that distinction matters far beyond the classroom.At the heart of economics and public policy lies a deeper question: how do we know if something actually works?The session explores:The difference between correlation and causation, and why it’s easy to confuse the twoHow economists use tools like hypothesis testing and p-values to separate signal from noiseWhat methods like difference-in-differences and regression discontinuity reveal about real-world impactHow policies are evaluated — from taxes to welfare programs — to determine whether they truly change outcomesThrough intuitive examples, the lecture moves beyond technical jargon to show how causal thinking shapes decisions that affect everyday life.About the SpeakerParush Arora is an Assistant Professor of Economics at Ashoka University. He completed his PhD in Economics from the University of California Irvine.His research focuses on econometrics, Bayesian inference, and empirical macroeconomics — fields that sit at the intersection of data, uncertainty, and decision-making. He holds a Bachelor’s degree in Economics from the University of Delhi and a Master’s from the Madras School of Economics.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

location.address: Siyah Arthouse, 3rd Floor 261, Plot 8, Westend Marg, Saidulajab, Saket, New Delhi, Delhi 110030, India

name: COAST TO COAST - Gruha x The House of Gonsalves
description: 🌿 A Curated Odia x Mangalorean Experience 🌿Gruha, a father-daughter Odia supper club, and The House of Gonsalves (HOG), a mother-daughter Mangalorean supper club come together for the very first time in Gurgaon to bring you something deeply personal—a Coast to Coast Lunch, where our homes, cultures, and traditions meet at one table.What began in our kitchens, with recipes passed down through generations, homemade masalas, and stories told over everyday meals, now comes together in a first-of-its-kind collaboration—two families, two coasts, one shared table. Every dish carries a piece of where we come from, the way we’ve grown up eating, and the love with which our families have always fed people.Hosted right here in Gurgaon, this intimate, community-led experiential table is more than just a meal—it’s an invitation into our worlds, shaped by stories, traditions, and home.✨ Expect familiar comfort, with unexpected and unique pairings.📆 Date: 19th April, Sunday📍 Location: Sector 52, Gurgaon⏰ Timing: 1-4 PM🌸 Experience Includes: This is a thoughtfully curated experience—a 4-course meal weaving Odia and Mangalorean cuisines, paired with two beverages, and ending with the comfort of Odia special tea and South Indian filter coffee, just like home.

name: The Morning Table: A Sunday Breakfast Gathering
description: The Brine Club is our way of bringing people together over food that’s meant to be eaten slowly — and building a community of like-minded people around the table.Every table is different, every menu changes — because I like letting the day decide where things go.Most of our gatherings are sit-down.The Morning Table is a Sunday breakfast brunch hosted at our home in Gurgaon. ( Exact location to be shared 24 hours before the event to maintain privacy)We’re putting together a menu of breakfast dishes from different parts of the world — the kind you usually only see online or in other cities. Think London’s viral honey caramel butter toast, fried chicken & waffles, and our own slightly indulgent mango brioche toast, along with a few other plates.One beverage of your choice is included. If you feel like another round, additional drinks can be ordered during the brunch on an à la carte basis.Everything will be served family-style, so the table eats together, passes plates around.The full menu is in the gallery.You’ll be sharing the table with 10 people — some strangers, some soon-to-be friends. If you’re someone who enjoys meeting new, interesting people over really good food, this might be your kind of Sunday morning.It’s relaxed, a little indulgent, and not the kind of breakfast you usually find around the city.

name: Paint Party - Sip & Paint Sundowner 🎨🌅
description: Sip & Paint Sundowner 🎨🌅 (11 April 2026 & 18 April 2026 | 4:30 PM onwards | Gurgaon)Not every plan needs loud music and crowded spaces.Some evenings are meant to be slower, softer… and more real.The Paint Party by HumSupper is an intimate, curated experience designed for people who want something different from the usual Gurgaon scene.As the sun sets, step into a cozy space where you’ll sip, paint, and connect, no pressure, no expectations. It’s about expression!Beyond the canvas, the evening unfolds into a thoughtfully curated supper and interactive moments that make it easy to connect with strangers, and maybe leave with stories, not just memories. name: A summer table from Odisha! - Pakhala Pop Up
description: Pakhala Pop-Up: A Summer Table from OdishaA Rosei Ghara x Kartik’s Kitchen CollaborationWe bring to the table a ritual from coastal Odisha — a meal designed for summer.Pakhala is not just fermented rice. It is comfort, cooling, seasonal wisdom, and memory served in a bowl.On the 19th of April, from 12 pm onwards, Rosei Ghara, in collaboration with Kartik’s Kitchen, invites you to experience an Odia summer table — thoughtfully curated, slow, and rooted in tradition.What to Expect:At the centre sits Pakhala — lightly fermented rice, cooling and probiotic-rich — accompanied by an abundant spread of classic summer sides. Expect crisp fries, smoky mash, leafy greens, lentil badis, seasonal vegetables, and carefully spiced accompaniments — each dish balancing texture, heat, tang, and crunch.Why It’s Special:• A rare opportunity to experience Odisha’s iconic summer meal in Delhi• A collaboration between two kitchens that celebrate regional Indian flavours• Limited seating to preserve the intimacy of the table• Hosted in a private residence in Chattarpur• A slow, immersive lunch designed around seasonality and traditionPakhala is typically eaten for lunch in Odia homes — and we invite you to experience it exactly as it is meant to be enjoyed: unhurried, generous, and shared.📍 Venue: Chattarpur, Delhi🗓️ Date : 19h April🕛 Time: 12 pm onwards✨ Limited seats | Community dining experienceCome curious. Come hungry.Leave with a new understanding of how Odisha does summer.

description: Journey of Hearts – Delhi Open Mic | Likhon Dil SeSome stories don’t need perfection.They just need a voice.Journey of Hearts is not just an open mic — it’s a space where emotions spill without filters, where broken sentences are still beautiful, and where silence is just as powerful as words.Hosted by Likhon Dil Se, this evening is for the ones who have loved deeply, lost quietly, healed slowly, and are still figuring it all out.Whether you’re a poet, storyteller, musician, or just someone who feels too much — this stage is yours.No judgments.No pretence.Just raw, honest expression.Come as you are.Leave a little lighter.

name: Shunaya
description: Shunya is a one-time New Moon sensory experience designed to help you pause, disconnect from external noise, and turn inward.Hosted at The Immersion Room, this intimate gathering blends sound, stillness, and guided awareness to create a deeply immersive journey. Rooted in the energy of the New Moon — a time symbolic of emptiness, reset, and quiet beginnings — Shunya invites you to experience the beauty of nothingness.The session is facilitated by Riti Yog and co-organised by Mehak Mahant, bringing together a thoughtful blend of mindfulness and intentional design. Through breathwork, meditation, and a carefully curated environment that gently engages all five senses — sound, sight, scent, touch, and subtle spatial awareness — participants are guided into a deeply personal inner space.Every element of the experience is designed to soften the mind and deepen presence, allowing you to fully arrive in the moment.With a limited group of 10–15 participants, the experience remains intimate, intentional, and deeply held.This is not just a session — it is a space to soften, release, and realign.Whether you’re seeking clarity, stillness, or simply a moment away from constant stimulation, Shunya offers a rare opportunity to slow down and reconnect with yourself.

name: The Sultanate’s Canvas: Sayyid Grace & Lodhi Might
description: In celebration of World Heritage Day!Don't just walk past history—walk through it. Whether you are a photography enthusiast, a curious wanderer, a history buff, or someone looking to see the city with fresh eyes, this canvas is waiting for you.To the casual stroller, Lodhi Gardens is a lush urban sanctuary. To the discerning eye, it is a battlefield of aesthetics—a sprawling stone canvas where two distinct dynasties etched their legacies in the twilight of the Delhi Sultanate.On World Heritage Day, we explore the evolution of architectural styles and influences that resulted in monuments which were distinct and reflected the temperament of their rulers. Join us for a curated heritage walk through Lodhi Gardens, where the shifting tides of the 15th-century Delhi Sultanate are etched into every dome and archway.

location.address: Lodhi Rd, Lodhi Gardens, Lodhi Estate, New Delhi, Delhi 110003, India

name: Nizamuddin Heritage walk on Sufism & Poetry
description: In the Sufi Bylanes of Nizamuddin — A Heritage Walk of Stories, Shrines & SilenceStep into the tender, time-worn lanes of Nizamuddin, where the fragrance of incense mingles with qawwali echoes and centuries of faith, love, and longing still breathe through stone and sky.Join us for a soulful heritage walk through the sacred precincts of Nizamuddin Dargah, as we trace the footsteps of saints, poets, patrons, and wanderers who made this neighbourhood a living archive of Delhi’s Sufi soul.On this journey, we will also pause at:✨ Ghalib’s Mazar — remembering the poet of longing and lament✨ Chausath Khamba — a marble pavilion of quiet grace and symmetry✨ Atagha Khan’s Tomb — a Mughal-era resting place of power and piety✨ Pateh Shah’s Dargah — where devotion meets deep serenity    & end at the Nizammuddin Dargah.Along the way, we’ll listen to stories of shrines and seekers, of migration and memory, of music, miracles, and mysticism — stories that still whisper through these alleys at dusk.

location.address: Bharat Scouts and Guides Marg, opposite Humayun Tomb Park, Nizamuddin, Hazrat Nizamuddin, National Zoological Park, Sundar Nagar, New Delhi, Delhi 110013, India

name: 1857 & Dilli : A heritage walk in Delhi Ridge
description: 1857 UPRISING & DILLI: (A heritage walk at the Mutiny Memorial & it's neighbourhood in Kamla Nehru Ridge )The year 1857 marked a turning point in Indian history. What began as a sepoy mutiny in Meerut soon transformed into a widespread rebellion against British colonial rule. Within days, the rebelling sepoys marched to Delhi, a city symbolic of imperial power, where they declared the aging Mughal emperor Bahadur Shah Zafar as the leader of the uprising.The Mutiny Memorial, now known as Ajitgarh, is a prominent monument located on the Delhi Ridge, near Kashmere Gate. It was constructed in 1863 by the British to honor their soldiers and officials who died during the 1857 uprising.Let’s explore the stories of the 1857 uprising and its impacts, at the Mutiny Memorial (a haunted site ) &  its neighborhood landmarks (Ashokan Pillar, Baoli, Pir Ghaib Hunting Lodge & the Hindu Rao Hospital)

location.address: Kamla Nehru Ridge, Civil Lines, Delhi, 110007, India

name: The World Of Work In Contemporary India
description: Join us for a lecture that asks a question that feels increasingly urgent:Why does economic growth no longer guarantee jobs?About the LectureAcross the world — and especially in India — stories of job loss, retrenchment, and precarious work have become almost routine.But these are not isolated disruptions. They are part of a deeper structural shift.This lecture examines the changing world of work through the lens of neoliberal capitalism — a phase that began gaining global momentum in the late 20th century and has since reshaped economies, institutions, and livelihoods. It explores how processes such as extractivism, land acquisition, resource grabbing, and intensified profit-seeking have transformed not just production, but the very conditions under which labour exists.The session explores:Why economic growth has become increasingly “jobless”The declining relationship between output and employment across sectorsHow neoliberal policy regimes reshape labour markets and livelihoodsWhat Marxian political economy reveals about accumulation and labour displacementDrawing on the idea of the “Absolute General Law of Capitalist Accumulation,” the lecture argues that labour displacement is not accidental, but intrinsic to the logic of capital itself,  driven by the constant expansion of productive forces and the pursuit of accumulation.At its core, this talk invites us to rethink what we call a “crisis” — not as a temporary breakdown, but as a systemic feature of how contemporary capitalism functions.About the SpeakerPraveen Jha is former Sukhamoy Chakravarty Chair Professor of Economics at Jawaharlal Nehru University, where he taught at the Centre for Economic Studies and Planning for several decades.His work spans political economy of development, with a focus on labour, agriculture, and education, alongside interests in natural resources, public finance, and the history of economic thought. He has also been affiliated with the Centre for Informal Sector and Labour Studies at JNU and has held visiting positions at institutions across Germany, China, South Africa, Zimbabwe, and Switzerland, including engagements with organisations such as the International Labour Organization.He has been associated with multiple research initiatives with UN agencies including the ILO, UNICEF, UNDP, and FAO, and is a founding editor of Agrarian South: Journal of Political Economy. With over twenty books and more than a hundred academic publications, his work remains central to debates on development, labour, and capitalism in India and beyond.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

location.address: E-17, Market Ln, Hauz Khas Market, Block E, Hauz Khas, Delhi, New Delhi, Delhi 110016, India

name: Can Fish Do Math? And Other Stories
description: Join us for a lecture that begins with a childlike question, and opens into one of the biggest scientific mysteries:How do brains actually work?About the LectureWe spend years in school learning how to read, write, calculate, and make decisions.But how our brains actually learn to do any of this remains surprisingly unclear.How do clusters of neurons produce language? How do they perform mathematical operations? How do they help us navigate social worlds, make choices, and decide something as simple as what to eat for lunch?This lecture turns to an unexpected set of teachers: animals. From fish that can estimate quantities, to bees that communicate through movement, to monkeys and birds that display complex cognitive abilities, the session explores what non-human minds can reveal about our own.This lecture explores:What animal behaviour can teach us about human cognitionHow different species process information, communicate, and make decisionsWhy studying simpler nervous systems helps us understand complex onesWhat remains unknown about how brains learn, think, and adaptBlending neuroscience, psychology, and evolutionary biology, this lecture invites us to rethink intelligence, not as something uniquely human, but as a spectrum of abilities shaped across species.At its core, it asks: if we still don’t fully understand our own minds, what might we learn by looking elsewhere?About the SpeakerBittu Kaveri Rajaraman is an Associate Professor of Biology and Psychology at Ashoka University. She received her PhD from Harvard University.Her work explores how brains, across species, process information, make decisions, and give rise to behaviour. Bringing together insights from biology and psychology, her research looks at cognition not as an abstract phenomenon, but as something rooted in neural systems shaped by evolution.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: 26th April: Blueprints for Anti-Caste Protagonism
description: Join us for a lecture that asks a difficult question:Why is it so hard to see anti-caste assertion, even when it is right in front of us?About the LectureWhat does it mean to represent a body that history has refused to recognise?This lecture turns to art as a site of both inquiry and resistance, examining how caste operates not only through direct violence, but through subtler, representational forms that shape what can be seen, known, and acknowledged.Drawing from works such as Mukta Salve Triptych (2023) and Tannerfilm (2024), the session traces how infrastructures of caste have historically made it nearly impossible for anti-caste voices and assertions to be positively registered. It examines the lingering continuities between colonial ethnography of the 1850s and contemporary modes of representation — both of which render the Dalit body as devoid of agency, even in moments of clear self-assertion.The talk explores:How caste operates through visual and cultural representationWhy anti-caste assertion is often rendered illegible or erasedThe continuity between colonial and contemporary ways of seeing Dalit subjectivityHow art can function as a space of critique, interruption, and re-imaginationAbout the SpeakerRahee Punyashloka is an artist, writer, and experimental filmmaker based between Bhubaneswar and New Delhi. His work spans visual art, film, and writing, with a focus on creating anti-caste imagery and emancipatory imaginaries for Dalit subjectivity.His works have been exhibited at institutions and festivals including Khoj Studios, the Museum of Art and Photography, Haus der Kulturen der Welt, the Tribeca Film Festival, and the International Film Festival Rotterdam. He is also known by his online moniker artedkar.All Grounds for Thought tickets include a ₹250 coupon redeemable on food and beverages at the venue — so you can listen, reflect, and stay for conversation.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who want learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: 19th April: The Default Citizen
description: The Default CitizenCaste, Minority, and the Idea of Citizenship in IndiaJoin us for a lecture that begins with a deceptively simple question:Who is seen as a natural citizen, and who must constantly prove they belong?About the LectureWe often think of citizenship as a legal category, defined by documents, rights, and recognition by the state.But citizenship is also a matter of imagination.Who is assumed to belong without question? And who is treated as a minority, required to negotiate, justify, and defend their place within the nation?This lecture explores the idea of citizenship in India through its entanglement with caste, majority, and minority. Drawing on B. R. Ambedkar’s reflections on the minority question, the session shifts the conversation beyond religion and numbers, asking what happens when caste is placed at the centre of how we understand belonging.The talk examines:Why minority in India cannot be reduced to numerical smallnessHow historical subordination and unequal representation shape political belongingThe emergence of a “default citizen” whose presence is seen as natural and unquestionedHow caste and majoritarian politics shape the very idea of citizenshipRather than treating citizenship as neutral or universal, this lecture reveals it as a deeply political construct, shaped by power, history, and the ongoing struggle for recognition.About the SpeakerVikas Kumar Moola is a doctoral researcher in the Humanities and Social Sciences at Indian Institute of Science Education and Research Mohali, where his work examines the conceptual and historical making of citizenship in India through the lens of caste and majority-minority relations.He holds a Master’s degree in Sociology from the University of Hyderabad and an M.Phil. in Social Sciences from Centre for Studies in Social Sciences Calcutta.Alongside his academic work, he is part of the editorial team at The Ambedkarian Chronicle, contributing to the development of an autonomous anti-caste knowledge institution. He has also worked in the development sector, combining socio-political analysis with grounded field research.Vikas is a Central Committee member of the All India Independent Scheduled Castes Association (AIISCA), where he is engaged in advancing Dalit rights, dignity, and collective assertion across regions and communities.All Grounds for Thought tickets include a ₹250 coupon redeemable on food and beverages at the venue — so you can listen, reflect, and stay for conversation.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Partition & Delhi heritage walk @Purana Qila
description: Join Us for Partition & Delhi heritage walk & Baithak at Purana Qila on 19th April,2026From nuclear proliferation to terrorism, the Partition of India continues to cast its shadow even today. Join us for a Baithak that seeks to understand the complex experiences of Partition—an event that remains deeply misunderstood both within and beyond the subcontinent. Through facts, poetry, literature, photographs, paintings, and personal belongings, we will revisit this significant chapter of history. Discover how Partition shaped Delhi’s culture and internal geography.Kuch Pal Purana Qila Ke Naam! (suitable for 10 years & above)*Date: 19th April, 2026 Time: 8.30 am* Duration: 2 hours approx * Language: A blend of Hindi, Urdu & English(Special invitation to senior citizens, as this session involves minimal walking!)* Baithak Host: Nitika-Meeting point: Purana Qila ticket counter --Nearest metro station: Supreme Court- Instagram: Darwesh_taleweavers

location.address: Mathura Rd, Near Delhi Zoo, J65V+RFH, Pragati Maidan, New Delhi, Delhi 110001, India

name: #26 Martian Tales From a Former NASA Engineer
description: #lecture25We’re bringing you Nimisha Mittal, a veteran space systems engineer with over 15 years of experience at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL). She led operations and testing for some of the world’s most ambitious robotic missions including the Cassini mission at Saturn, three of NASA's five Mars rovers, the Insight Mars lander, and most recently the NASA-ISRO SAR mission, NISAR. After a career of exploring the solar system, she's just completed her own mission of relocating to India. With engineering degrees from UT Austin and Stanford, she's now turning her attention from exploring other worlds to helping build the future of the Indian space industry.Her talk, Martian Tales from a Former NASA Engineer, dives into the thrill of driving robots across another planet and the bigger question of why Mars captivates us so deeply. Is it the search for life, the dream of a second home, or something even more profound? With insider stories from her time on the Mars mission, she takes us through what a day on the Red Planet really looks like, the challenges of remote space exploration, and even what it takes to earn a Martian Driver’s License.Come find out, and bring your questions — from whether humans can truly settle on Mars to what snacks power a NASA engineer through a 12-hour shift.All of this, over beer or mocktail at The Piano Man, Eldeco Centre! 🍻About the LecturerNimisha Mittal is a veteran space systems engineer with over 15 years of experience at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL), where she led operations and testing for some of the world’s most ambitious robotic missions including the Cassini mission at Saturn, three of NASA's five Mars rovers, the Insight Mars lander, and most recently the NASA-ISRO SAR mission NISAR. After a career of exploring the solar system, she's just completed her own mission of relocating to India. With engineering degrees from UT Austin and Stanford, she's now turning her attention from exploring other worlds to helping build the future of the Indian space industry.Book now & develop your own pint of view! 🍻📌 Note: Alcohol will be served only to guests aged 25 and above. Non-alcoholic options will be available. Attendance implies consent to being filmed for event documentation.

name: The Procrastireaders : Books & Coffee Tasting
description: The Procrastireaders: Books & Coffee TastingOverviewIf you have been wanting to get back to reading but find it hard to make the time at home, this is for you. The Procrastireaders is a dedicated space to finally open that book you have been meaning to finish. It is a slow paced morning designed for quiet reading and great coffee in the company of others who are doing the same.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community led initiative dedicated to creating safe and mindful spaces. Through intimate workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities from mental well being to personal growth with empathy and shared human connection.What to expectBring Your Own Book: Whether it is a novel you started months ago or a new non fiction find, bring along whatever you are currently reading.Dedicated Reading Time: Enjoy a block of quiet, uninterrupted time to sink into your book without the distractions of chores or your phone.Shared Atmosphere: The comfort of reading alongside a small group of people who are also prioritizing their love for books.Coffee & Chat: A low pressure chance to talk about what you are reading (or why you have been procrastinating) over a coffee at Unlocked.Details🗓️ Date: 25th April (Saturday)⏰ Time: 11:30 AM📍 Location: Unlocked, 32nd Avenue🎟️ Contribution: ₹700 (Includes Coffee)

location.address: 32nd Avenue, Sector 15 Part 2, Sector 15, Gurugram, Haryana 122001, India

name: Open SketchBook
description: Open SketchBook: You don’t need to leave the city to feel grounded.OverviewOur core belief is that you do not need to leave the city to feel grounded. In a world where everyone is distracted, we pause as a group to give our attention to one shared thing. Sketching becomes the medium to notice small, living details in urban spaces that we usually rush through.You do not need to be an artist to enjoy sketching. Open SketchBook is a session for anyone who wants to explore drawing as a way to relax and observe the world. It is about the process of putting pencil to paper rather than the final masterpiece.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community led initiative dedicated to creating safe and mindful spaces. Through intimate workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities from mental well being to personal growth with empathy and shared human connection.What to expectGuided Observation: Learn simple ways to look at objects or spaces and translate them into lines and shapes.Creative Freedom: A judgment free zone where you can experiment with sketching without worrying about "doing it right."Tactile Experience: Use your new diary and pencil kit to capture what you see in the moment.Relaxed Environment: Enjoy a late afternoon at Greenr with a small group of people who just want to create and connect.Details🗓️ Date: 19th April (Sunday)⏰ Time: 5:00 PM📍 Location: Greenr, Golf Course Ext Road🎟️ Contribution: ₹700 (Includes an A6 diary and pencil kit)

location.address: First Floor, Grand View High Street, Golf Course Ext Rd, Sector 58, Gurugram, Haryana 122098, India

name: The Listening Room
description: The Listening Room: A Space for Deep ListeningOverviewIn a world that is always talking, we rarely get the chance to truly listen. The Listening Room is a unique session dedicated to the art of being present. It is a space to share, to hear others, and to practice the kind of attention that builds real connection and empathy.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community led initiative dedicated to creating safe and mindful spaces. Through intimate workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities from mental well being to personal growth with empathy and shared human connection.What to expectActive Listening: Practice being fully present while others speak without the need to interrupt or give immediate advice.Shared Stories: A safe environment to share your own thoughts or experiences and feel truly heard by a small group.Quiet Reflection: Moments of silence to process what has been shared and to notice how deep listening changes our perspective.Supportive Connection: A morning at Greenr focused on human presence and the simple power of witnessing each other’s stories.Details🗓️ Date: 19th April (Sunday)⏰ Time: 11:00 AM📍 Location: Greenr, Golf Course Ext Road🎟️ Contribution: ₹700

location.address: First Floor, Grand View High Street, Golf Course Ext Rd, Sector 58, Gurugram, Haryana 122098, India

name: The Art of Folding
description: The Art of Folding: A Morning of Origami and FocusOverviewThis session is inspired by the cherry blossom season in Japan, symbolizing new beginnings and change. Participants will learn to create origami cherry blossoms and simple floral arrangements in a guided and beginner friendly environment.There is a unique kind of calm that comes from turning a simple square of paper into something beautiful. The Art of Folding is a hands on workshop dedicated to the meditative practice of origami. It is about slowing down, focusing on the present moment, and finding patience in every fold.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community led initiative dedicated to creating safe and mindful spaces. Through intimate workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities from mental well being to personal growth with empathy and shared human connection.What to expectStep by Step Guidance: Learn the foundational folds of origami to create intricate paper designs from scratch.Tactile Focus: Use your hands to engage in a process that naturally quiets the mind and improves concentration.Patience and Precision: A chance to practice a slow craft where the journey of folding is just as rewarding as the final piece.Creative Connection: Share a peaceful morning at Cha & Co with a small group of people who appreciate the beauty of simple things.Details🗓️ Date: 18th April (Saturday)⏰ Time: 11:30 AM📍 Location: Cha & Co, Ireo Grand View High Street🎟️ Contribution: ₹700 (Includes Origami Paper + Materials)

name: ALT EFF Film Club x GuruJal Gurugram | 17th April
description: If you are tired of the outrage cycle, doomscrolling collapse, and the looming AI takeover narrative. If you have caught yourself going numb just to cope. We see you.All Living Things Environmental Film Festival is back with the third season of Film Club. A monthly meet up across 10 cities over 5 months, orbiting environmental cinema that cracks you open without trapping you in doom and gloom.It is a room full of people choosing to show up in cities whose infrastructure persuades you to stay home, choosing to sit together, watch together, and turn private anxiety into collective imagination. Messy. Human. Honest conversation.With snacks. Always snacks.To become a Film Club Member, continue with the regular booking process. The membership option will appear after you select the venue and date.APRIL CURATION: "The Climate Baby Dilemma"Synopsis:"The Climate Baby Dilemma" by filmmaker Victoria Lean follows science writer Britt Wray as she explores the decision to have children amid climate change. Over three years, she engages with activists and parents, reflecting generational concerns. Featuring Sarain Fox and Severn Suzuki, the film offers insights on transforming climate anxiety into action.PROGRAMME17th April 2026, Friday7:00 - 9:00 PM: The Climate Baby Dilemma | Victoria Lean | Canada | 44 mins | English | 2022All activities, including discussions and refreshments, will occur between 7:00 and 9:00 PM.What's Included?Film screeningGroup discussionLight refreshmentsCommunity spaceStudent Discount50% off for students with a valid ID (& must bring ID to the screening).CODE: ALTSTUDENTABOUT ALT EFFAll Living Things Environmental Film Festival (ALT EFF) is the world’s largest decentralised film festival and a premiere showcase for environmental cinema.Across its annual festival, monthly Film Club, schools initiatives, film fund, and year round curations, ALT EFF nurtures stories grounded in care, collective responsibility, stewardship, and an abiding love for the natural world.Spanning countries, genres, languages, and forms, it brings together stories reimagining our relationship with all living things.

location.address: 6/9, F-2, Block F, DLF Phase 1, Sector 26A, Gurugram, Haryana 122002, India

organizer.name: All Living Things Environmental Film Festival

name: TAI CHI WITH RASHID Drop In Classes
description: Join the Tai Chi Practice Circle with sensei Rashid Ansari for cultivating wellness, calmness, grounding and inner balance with the transformative practices of Tai Chi and Qi Gong. Held every Sunday at Lilanoor Centre. Hosted by IT'S ALL GOOD.Cost : Rs. 1500/- per session (Student Rs. 600/-)***Both Tai Chi and Qi Gong offer a unique perspective of being a Participant and Observer simultaneously.Tai chi, often called Meditation in Motion, is an incredibly potent system of mind body synergy and a block and tackle system, working on the joints and angles of the body.regulates blood pressure and hypertension.the different movements work on the different organs and glands, helping and energising them to work at optimum potency.strengthens the muscles and tendons, aligns the skeletal and neuromuscular system.helps to deal with anxiety and mental health issues.can be practiced by anybody - young and old alike.Rashid Ansari has been practicing/teaching the martial arts , dance, movement, choreography and holistic health/wellness for over 50 years now, both in India and various parts of the world. In addition to martial arts, Ansari is a contemporary dance instructor and choreographer. He has explored physical theatre and theatre movement, integrating these disciplines into his teaching methodology. He founded and serves as the artistic director of Mobius Strip, a dance theatre company, and Earth Rhythms, a world music ensemble. He also conducts classes and workshops in “Dance your Senses - The 5 Rhythms.” A organic movement methodology based on the individual’s own intrinsic sense of movement and emotions - as a means of finding one’s own movement and dealing with physical, emotional and intellectual blocks.***IT'S ALL GOOD | The Science and Art of JoyMany people feel stuck, exasperated, low, or just plain bored in their lives. We provide a platform for fun and engaging experiences that foster insight and empowerment.Connect | Learn | Thrive!INSTAGRAM
